articles.title: Footballers more likely to suffer depression and anxiety - study
articles.summary: A new study finds evidence of reduced brain volume in former elite footballers, who also report difficulties with thinking skills and decision-making.

articles.title: Leigh stay on course for Play-offs after Castleford win
articles.summary: Highlights of the Super League match between Leigh Leopards and Castleford Tigers.

articles.title: Colourful, controversial and significant - ex-Chelsea and Leeds owner Bates dies
articles.summary: Former Chelsea and Leeds owner Ken Bates, who has died aged 94, was a colourful and controversial figure - but also a significant one for English football, writes Phil McNulty.

articles.title: Holders St Mirren start with win as Linlithgow cause shock
articles.summary: Holders St Mirren start the defence of the Premier Sports Cup with a comfortable 4-0 win over Dumbarton as second-tier Greenock Morton suffer a shock defeat at home to Lowland League champions Linlith...

articles.title: Coventry City sign winger Tchaouna from Burnley
articles.summary: Premier League Coventry City signed winger Loum Tchaouna from relegated Burnley for an undisclosed fee.

articles.title: Wales defender Lockyer signs for Melbourne Victory
articles.summary: Wales defender Tom Lockyer agrees a deal to join Australian A-League side Melbourne Victory.

articles.title: Edwards leaves football chief role with Liverpool owners FSG
articles.summary: Liverpool owners Fenway Sports Group announce the departure of Michael Edwards, the company's chief executive of football.

articles.title: Spain beat Germany to win U19 Euros in Wrexham
articles.summary: Spain claim the Uefa Men's Under-19 Championship for the 10th time with a 2-0 victory over Germany at Wrexham's Racecourse Ground.

articles.title: Noskova recovers to win maiden Wimbledon title after six Championship points
articles.summary: Linda Noskova sealed her maiden Grand Slam title as she became the third Czech women's Wimbledon champion in four years with victory over compatriot Karolina Muchova on Saturday - but only after overc...

articles.title: Scotland's late comeback falls short against Springboks
articles.summary: Scotland produced another big performance against top-tier opposition but their wait for a first win in South Africa continues after they went down 42-28 in a thrilling Nations Championship showdown i...

articles.title: Tuchel angry at 'lucky' England - but Bellingham defends players
articles.summary: England head coach Thomas Tuchel is praised by former England players for saying his players "got lucky" in World Cup quarter-final win over Norway - but match-winner Jude Bellingham disagrees.

articles.title: 'We should all be excited' - Messi set for first England meeting
articles.summary: Lionel Messi is set for his first match against England after Argentina beat Switzerland in the World Cup quarter-finals.

articles.title: 'Animals', Hand of God and Beckham - Argentina and England's World Cup rivalry
articles.summary: As England and Argentina prepare to go head-to-head in Wednesday's World Cup semi-final, BBC Sport looks at their footballing rivalry.

articles.title: Bellingham reaching heights of World Cup legends with Messi next
articles.summary: Jude Bellingham again dazzles England and the World Cup with his brilliance, matching some records of the all-time greats.

articles.title: Did ball hit spidercam before England goal? Snicko says not - Norway unsure
articles.summary: Norway's World Cup campaign ends in a quarter-final defeat by England, but they leave frustrated, arguing that Jude Bellingham's equaliser should not have counted.

articles.title: Senegal sack manager Thiaw in wake of World Cup exit
articles.summary: Senegal sack manager Pape Thiaw in the aftermath of the country's exit at the last-32 stage of the 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Did spider cam impact England's opener?
articles.summary: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Wayne Rooney, Ellen White and Micah Richards look at whether the spider cam had an impact in Jude Bellingham's first goal for England against Norway.

articles.title: England 'got lucky' with disallowed goal
articles.summary: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Wayne Rooney and Ellen White believe England "got lucky" after Torbjorn Heggem's second-half goal was ruled out for a foul on Elliott Anderson by Erling Haaland.

articles.title: Merson: Teams win leagues, X-factor wins World Cups - and Bellingham has it
articles.summary: Paul Merson says Jude Bellingham has the X-factor needed to help England win the World Cup.

articles.title: 'Man of all the moments' - Bellingham gives England the lead
articles.summary: Jude Bellingham scores his second goal of the game to give England a 2-1 lead over Norway during extra time of their 2026 World Cup quarter-final in Miami.

articles.title: All the goals from England's 2026 World Cup campaign so far
articles.summary: As England gear up to face Norway at the Miami Stadium for the World Cup quarter-finals, re-live all the goals that got Thomas Tuchel's side there.

articles.title: When the World Cup finishes, the football continues on BBC Sport
articles.summary: When the World Cup ends we'll still have loads of great content every day as we build up to the new football season.

articles.title: France into semi-finals after comfortable win over Morocco
articles.summary: Kylian Mbappe scores his eighth goal of this World Cup to make up for an earlier penalty miss with Ousmane Dembele adding a second as France prove too good for Morocco and move into the semi-finals.
